L1.head: Metal ions
L1.claim: Metal ions influence enzyme structure through coordination geometries

L1.head: Disulfide bonds
L1.claim: Disulfide bonds typically reduce enzyme activity

L1.head: Quantum chemical calculations
L1.claim: Quantum calculations can predict optimal metal-disulfide configurations

L1.head: Supervised machine learning
L1.claim: ML integrates predictions into hierarchical networks

L1.head: hierarchical modular networks
L1.claim: Optimized networks lead to 20%+ improvement in stability and activity

L2.head: Molecular dynamics simulations
L2.claim: MD simulations capture dynamic PET polymer conformations

L2.head: Graph neural networks
L2.claim: GNNs learn temporal patterns from MD simulations

L2.head: transient binding pockets
L2.claim: Targeting predicted pockets enables 10x activity improvement

L2.head: Protein surface charge evolution
L2.claim: Surface charges evolve non-randomly under fitness landscapes

L2.head: Salt bridges
L2.claim: Salt bridges affect both stability and catalytic activity

L2.head: Co-evolution mapping
L2.claim: Charge co-evolution mapping buffers destabilizing mutations

L3.head: Dynamic squeezing index
L3.claim: Squeezing index perturbs enzyme conformations under pressure

L3.head: residue optimization
L3.claim: Optimized variants maintain activity under 500+ bar

L3.head: Epistasis patterns
L3.claim: Epistasis patterns differ between enzyme families

L3.head: universal design principles
L3.claim: Framework predicts epistatic interactions in novel folds
